notes: Models the pre-session proc-PID → UUID-1 → /clear → UUID-2 lifecycle precisely. session_birth matches the proc-PID's ready (first event); v1_session_handoff matches the UUID-keyed ready; all v1 phases pinned to v1_session_handoff via same_session_as; old_session_ended requires transcript_removed for THAT exact UUID (rejecting the pre-session removal); v2_session_birth requires new_session: true — the strongest observable proof of 'content was reset'. Issue #169 fixed in daemon at commit 56c4667: PIDManager's same-PID cleanup now records a KindTranscriptRemoved lifecycle event in addition to the existing dashboard broadcast. Pass rate is now 9/9.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s
After the first prompt lands, the UUID-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID
First user prompt drives ready → working on the UUID session
First turn completes back to ready (same UUID)
Spec: 'Old session disappears' — after /clear, the daemon must emit transcript_removed for the FIRST UUID (NOT for the pre-session proc-<PID>)
Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — a fresh UUID never seen before; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'content was reset'
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
